title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy, authentic recipe. Creamy, rich, and bursting with flavor – perfect for any night!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and a side of naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our authentic Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) recipe.

description: Experience the iconic flavors of Northern India with our Classic Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani. This beloved dish features tender chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce, infused with aromatic spices and a touch of butter. It is a truly comforting and indulgent meal that captures the essence of traditional Indian cuisine.

ingredients.notes: or Kashmiri chili powder for color
soaked in hot water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, red chili powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight for best results.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter and 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until browned and slightly cooked through. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee and 1 tablespoon of butter. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Base
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, kasuri methi, and sugar. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to break down. Stir occasionally.

instructions.title: Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the tomato mixture to a blender along with the soaked cashews (drained). Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky sauce, you can pass it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step ensures a luxurious, restaurant-style texture.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the blended sauce to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces, heavy cream, and the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Stir gently to combine. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to finish cooking and absorb the rich flavors of the sauce. Adjust salt and sugar to taste if needed.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti. This classic Butter Chicken is perfect for a comforting family meal or entertaining guests.

tips: For the most tender chicken, marinate the chicken overnight. This allows the yogurt and spices to deeply penetrate the meat.
Always blend the sauce until perfectly smooth. This is key to achieving the signature velvety texture of restaurant-style Butter Chicken.
Finish with a swirl of fresh cream and a final sprinkle of crushed kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves) right before serving for an authentic aroma and flavor boost.
article: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
